# hfo — Hugging Face · Ollama, in your terminal

Fullscreen terminal UI and headless CLI that turns any Hugging Face GGUF
repository into a running, hardware-tuned Ollama model — in one Enter
keystroke. Cross-OS (Windows, macOS, Linux). Strict TypeScript. MIT
licensed. No telemetry, no accounts, no subscriptions. Version 0.1.0.

Website: https://hfo.carrillo.app/
Repository: https://github.com/carrilloapps/hfo
npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/hfo-cli

## What hfo does

Running open-weight large language models locally via Ollama is powerful
but punishingly repetitive. You browse Hugging Face, pick a repo, eyeball
the quant table, guess which quant fits your VRAM, read the model card for
temperature / top_p hints, download the `.gguf`, hand-write a Modelfile,
`ollama create` it, tune env vars (OLLAMA_FLASH_ATTENTION,
OLLAMA_KV_CACHE_TYPE, …) differently on every OS, back it up, restore it,
manage a dozen tags, and hand it off to Claude Code or Codex.

hfo collapses that into a single session. It reads your rig, scores every
quant in the repo against your VRAM and RAM, parses the model card for
recommended sampling parameters, and — on the happy path — installs a
ready-to-run model with one Enter keystroke. Every TUI capability has a
non-interactive flag so you can script the same pipeline from CI, cron, or
an editor plugin.

## Install

**One-liner scripts (no Node required)**

macOS / Linux — detects OS and architecture, installs to /usr/local/bin or
~/.local/bin:
```
curl -fsSL https://hfo.carrillo.app/install.sh | sh
```

Windows PowerShell — installs to %LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\hfo and adds it
to user PATH. No admin prompt required:
```
irm https://hfo.carrillo.app/install.ps1 | iex
```

Both scripts always pull the latest GitHub Release binary for your
platform. Override with HFO_VERSION=v0.2.0 (or $env:HFO_VERSION) to pin a
specific tag, or HFO_INSTALL_DIR=/some/dir to relocate. Binaries are built
and published only when a v*.*.* tag is pushed, never on regular commits.

**Package managers**

Pick the one you already have. All four ship the same hfo-cli package from
the npm registry:
```
npm  i   -g hfo-cli
pnpm add -g hfo-cli
yarn global add hfo-cli
bun  add -g hfo-cli
```

Requires Node.js 20 or newer. Ollama is optional — hfo will install it for
you via winget, brew, or the official shell script if it isn't already on
your PATH.

**Standalone binaries**

Each tagged release on GitHub publishes self-contained executables:
- hfo-linux-x64
- hfo-linux-arm64
- hfo-macos-x64 (Intel)
- hfo-macos-arm64 (Apple Silicon)
- hfo-win-x64.exe

Download from the Releases page, make it executable, and put it on your
PATH. No Node runtime required.

## Quick start

```
# Explore — fullscreen dashboard: hardware, capacity, picks, live metrics
hfo

# Install a specific repo (opens the Install tab pre-filled)
hfo bartowski/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct-GGUF

# Print hardware profile, capacity score, tier, picks, HF search URLs
hfo --view

# Install headlessly (quick-confirm screen auto-accepts on Enter)
hfo bartowski/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-GGUF --code

# Launch a coding agent wired to a local model
hfo --launch claude --model llama3.1:8b

# Back up a model folder
hfo --backup qwen2-5-coder-7b:q4-k-m

# Restore later
hfo --restore ~/.config/hfo/backups/2026-04-23_11-48-25/qwen2-5-coder-7b_q4-k-m.zip
```

Models download to the current working directory by default. Override with
`--dir` / `-d` or persist a default via the Settings tab (`modelDir`).

## CLI reference

Every TUI capability ships a headless flag that prints plain text and
exits non-zero on failure.

- `hfo`: open the fullscreen TUI (Dashboard tab)
- `hfo <hf-url-or-repo>`: open the TUI pre-filled on the Install tab
- `hfo --help` / `-h`: print the full flag reference
- `hfo --version`: print version, license, author
- `hfo --view`: print hardware, capacity score, tier, picks, HF search URLs
- `hfo --list`: list installed models plus orphan GGUFs on disk
- `hfo --tune`: persist the ~90% capacity Ollama env profile, restart daemon
- `hfo --backup <tag>`: create a .zip backup of the model folder
- `hfo --restore <zip>`: extract a backup and re-register with Ollama
- `hfo --delete <tag>`: remove the tag from Ollama (shallow)
- `hfo --delete <tag> --deep`: remove tag AND delete files on disk
- `hfo --launch <integration>`: run `ollama launch` with the given model
- `hfo --tab <name>`: open the TUI on dashboard | models | install | tune | help | settings

Flags:
- `--dir` / `-d`: base directory for install file browser (default: settings.modelDir → cwd)
- `--token` / `-t`: Hugging Face token for gated/private repos (or HF_TOKEN env)
- `--code` / `-c`: mark the install as code-specialized (adjusts SYSTEM prompt)
- `--ctx`: force context size (default: auto — 8192 if fits in VRAM, else 4096)
- `--model`: model tag to pass to --launch
- `--deep`: deep-delete alongside --delete
- `--no-fullscreen`: disable the alt-screen buffer

## Keyboard reference

Global:
- 1–6: jump to a tab
- Tab / Shift+Tab: cycle tabs
- , : open Settings
- ?: open Help
- Ctrl+H: open the author's homepage
- q / Ctrl+C: quit (restores scrollback)

Dashboard:
- Alt+Up / Alt+Down: focus / cycle panels
- Esc / b: return to the 4-panel grid
- Enter: open selected link in browser
- r: force refresh

Models:
- Up / Down: navigate rows
- L: launch integration with selected model
- G: launch menu without preset
- I: reinstall an orphan (regenerates Modelfile if missing)
- B: create a .zip backup
- d: remove tag only
- Alt+d: remove tag AND delete files
- r: refresh

Install (quick-confirm):
- Enter: install now with recommended defaults
- O: change target directory
- C: customize Modelfile parameters
- Esc: back to the quant picker

Tune:
- Up/Down: navigate rows
- Left/Right: cycle enum values
- E: edit freely
- D: default
- S: suggested
- R: reset to suggested
- X: reset to defaults
- A: apply

Settings:
- Enter: open dropdown (themes or 20 languages, searchable)

## Privacy, security, cost

hfo makes exactly two kinds of external requests, both to Hugging Face's
public, unauthenticated API:
- GET https://huggingface.co/api/models/<repo>/tree/main
- GET https://huggingface.co/<repo>/resolve/main/<file>

Everything else — nvidia-smi, systeminformation, ollama ps, ollama create,
ollama launch — runs locally. No telemetry. No accounts. Gated or private
repos use a standard HF_TOKEN environment variable or the --token flag.

The Ollama install helpers (winget install, brew install, the official
shell script) run only when the user explicitly opts in from the TUI's
Ollama installer overlay.

All costs are zero: no subscriptions, no API keys, no rate-limited tiers.

## Features at a glance

- Fullscreen TUI with six tabs: Dashboard, Models, Install, Tune, Help, Settings
- Hardware scoring: every GGUF graded 0–100 against usable VRAM + RAM
- HF model-card parameter parser: overlays recommended temp/top_p/top_k/ctx
- Quick-confirm install: Enter to install, O to change dir, C to customize
- Orphan reinstall: tags removed from Ollama but GGUFs still on disk get recovered
- Zip backup + restore with level-9 compression and metadata sidecar
- Deep delete: d removes tag only, Alt+d also wipes the tracked directory
- Launch integrations: Claude Code, Cline, Codex, Copilot CLI, Droid, Hermes, Kimi, OpenCode, OpenClaw, Pi, VS Code
- ~90% capacity tuner for OLLAMA_FLASH_ATTENTION, OLLAMA_KV_CACHE_TYPE, OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE, OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL, OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS, OLLAMA_MAX_QUEUE
- 7 themes (Dark, Light, Dracula, Solarized Dark/Light, Nord, Monokai)
- 20 languages (English, Spanish, Chinese, Hindi, Arabic, Portuguese, Bengali, Russian, Japanese, German, French, Korean, Italian, Turkish, Vietnamese, Indonesian, Polish, Dutch, Thai, Ukrainian)

## Configuration

Settings file location:
- Windows: %APPDATA%\hfo\settings.json
- macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/hfo/settings.json
- Linux: $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/hfo/settings.json (defaults to ~/.config/hfo/)

Schema:
```ts
interface Settings {
  theme: 'dark' | 'light' | 'dracula' | 'solarized-dark' | 'solarized-light' | 'nord' | 'monokai';
  language: 'en' | 'es' | 'zh' | 'hi' | 'ar' | 'pt' | 'bn' | 'ru' | 'ja' | 'de'
          | 'fr' | 'ko' | 'it' | 'tr' | 'vi' | 'id' | 'pl' | 'nl' | 'th' | 'uk';
  refreshIntervalMs: number;       // Dashboard polling cadence
  defaultSort: 'trending' | 'downloads' | 'likes7d' | 'modified';
  defaultCodeMode: boolean;
  modelDir: string | null;         // null = current working directory at launch
  useAltScreen: boolean;
  installations: Installation[];   // { tag, dir, repoId, quant, installedAt }
}
```

Backups live under <configDir>/hfo/backups/<timestamp>/<slug>.zip with a
sidecar .metadata.json containing tag, repo, quant, byte counts, and
compression ratio.

## FAQ

**Is hfo free?** Yes. MIT-licensed open source with no subscriptions, no
accounts, and no telemetry. It only talks to the public Hugging Face API
and your local Ollama daemon.

**Which operating systems does hfo support?** Windows, macOS, and Linux.
The npm package is cross-OS; standalone binaries for each platform are
published on the GitHub Releases page when a version is tagged.

**Do I need to install Ollama first?** No. If Ollama isn't on your PATH,
hfo will offer to install it using the platform-appropriate method —
winget on Windows, Homebrew on macOS, or the official shell script on
Linux. You always confirm before anything is installed.

**How does hfo choose which GGUF to recommend?** Every quant in a Hugging
Face repo is scored 0–100 against your detected VRAM and RAM headroom.
The tool reads the model's README for recommended temperature, top_p,
top_k, repeat_penalty, min_p and context size, then synthesises a tuned
Modelfile. On the quick-confirm screen you can accept with Enter, change
the install directory with O, or customise the Modelfile parameters with C.

**Can I launch Claude Code or Codex with a local model?** Yes. Press L on
any installed model and hfo will run ollama launch against the chosen
integration — Claude Code, Cline, Codex, Copilot CLI, Droid, Hermes, Kimi,
OpenCode, OpenClaw, Pi, or VS Code — with that model as the backend.

**Where are my model files stored?** By default under the directory you
launched hfo from (process.cwd()). Each model lives in
<baseDir>/<repo-slug>/<quant>/, with the GGUF, a tuned Modelfile, and hfo
records the tag→directory mapping so Alt+d (deep delete) and B (backup)
know what to operate on. Set a persistent default in the Settings tab
(modelDir).
